ogTitle: Stirling Estate vs Stirling Range National Park: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Stirling Estate and Stirling Range National Park.
heading: Stirling Estate vs Stirling Range National Park — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Stirling Estate, Western Australia and Stirling Range National Park, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Stirling Estate has a lower overall climate risk score (41/100) compared to Stirling Range National Park (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Stirling Estate or Stirling Range National Park safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Stirling Estate has a flood risk score of 60/100, while Stirling Range National Park scores 54/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Stirling Estate scores 35/100 for bushfire risk. Stirling Range National Park scores 49/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Stirling Estate or Stirling Range National Park?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Stirling Estate has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.
